_meta.source: memory doc + workspace docs + stakeholders + linked tasks + context_store.current_focus
_meta.note: Point-in-time analysis. Regenerate when refreshing the review. P0s authored directly; P1s via parallel analysis agents.

claude-kyc-agent.tldr: A 0-to-1 strategic bet, just promoted to P0. It's a vendor-agnostic KYC orchestration layer built ON TOP of foundation-model KYC agents (Anthropic KYC Screener / Google ADK) — 'use what they built, don't rebuild or compete' — so Payoneer can swap providers while owning extraction logic, compliance, and audit. The early signal is strong: Claude-based extraction hit 91% field accuracy vs Persona's 65% on 45 native-validated Japanese docs. Yonatan's Jun 3 call is decisive: stop perfecting per-language accuracy and ship it — the bottleneck is plumbing (a doc-access service), not extraction quality. Shilhav Ben-David now leads day-to-day; near-term play is Post-rep QA on eBay flows, in front of reps within weeks.

signals.label: Early result
signals.value: Claude extraction 91% vs Persona 65% on 45 native-validated Japanese docs (v2 prompt, +26.5pp)

signals.label: Strategic framing
signals.value: Agnostic layer over Anthropic KYC Screener / Google ADK — swap providers, own extraction/compliance/audit. Not a model, not a competitor.

signals.label: Decisive redirect
signals.value: Yonatan (Jun 3): deployment & placement > per-language accuracy — the bottleneck is plumbing, not %

signals.label: Near-term bottleneck
signals.value: Doc-access service (Omer): surface docs → agent → report in <3 min. Post-rep QA is the fastest first cell (~nothing required)

signals.label: Team / hygiene gaps
signals.value: Shilhav leads but no assigned_agent; Shilhav/Yaniv/Sivan/Omer not in people table; no target_date

signals.label: Open risks
signals.value: Sample still small (45 JP docs); US path gated (UK easier); screening vendor TBC; reproduce Yaniv's audited set as credibility benchmark

recommendation: Hold the deployment-first line: get Post-rep QA in front of reps on eBay flows within the upcoming office weeks — a rep seeing the UI beats another accuracy point.
Unblock the doc-access service with Omer now; it's the real bottleneck. The <3-min case→report is the demo that sells the whole thing — escalate if Omer is blocked.
Reproduce Yaniv's official audited set as the credibility benchmark before broadening — 45 Japanese docs is a signal, not proof.
Connect this explicitly to the vendor-optimization portfolio decision: 91% vs 65% directly pressures Persona OCR spend — these two threads should inform each other.

ai-native-team-structure.tldr: The thinking is done and genuinely strong — operating model published, hiring process designed, recruitment opened. But execution has stalled: no one is hired (lead PM candidate Denis declined in April), the engineer role is still undefined, and no pod has been stood up. The risk now is the exact thing Yonatan said he didn't want — a brilliant manifesto that gets forgotten. The bias needs to shift from research to commitment.

signals.label: Blocker

recommendation: Make the recommendation to Liat now — a concrete first-pod proposal (domain + composition + infra prerequisites + success criteria), not a perfected transition playbook. Force a go/no-go.
Unblock the engineer role this week: a 1-hour decision with Tal/Tomer. It has been "pending" for 3 months and it gates the whole pod.
Refresh the memory doc and confirm the live recruitment pipeline — 47 days dark on a P0 is itself the signal.
Apply the initiative's own thesis to itself: "building the wrong thing perfectly is the new risk" — commit to a real pod before more research.

air-squared.tldr: An initiative whose entire premise is speed has been static for ~70 days. It's still pre-staffing and pre-first-experiment: the core team (2 devs + 1 PM) isn't hired, no experiment has shipped, and there's no throughput target. For a "prove by doing" mandate, the gap between the promise (ship fast) and the reality (nothing shipped) is the headline risk — and Oren already rejected the slower infra-framing once.

signals.label: Contradiction
signals.value: "Speed above all" initiative — yet ~70d with no experiment run

signals.label: Staffing
signals.value: Core team (2 devs + 1 PM) not identified; deck to Oren for headcount is the gate

signals.label: Resource lever
signals.value: Gaurav's India team (~50 eng, ~2h every morning) available but unused

signals.label: History
signals.value: Oren rejected the prior infra/platform framing — mandate is execution, not enablement

signals.label: Framework
signals.value: Discovery → MVP → Run 3wk → Keep/Toss → Scale defined; no "Beat" target set

signals.label: Staleness
signals.value: Last update ~70d ago (Mar 25). Oldest workspace doc of the three P0s.

recommendation: Run one experiment now with borrowed hands — use Gaurav's India morning capacity rather than waiting for dedicated headcount. Pick a single bounded problem and run Discovery→MVP in ~2 weeks.
Make the result, not the deck, the resourcing argument to Oren — a shipped internal experiment beats another platform pitch (which he already rejected).
Set a minimal "Beat" target (e.g. 1 experiment to Keep/Toss per N weeks) so throughput is measurable.
If it genuinely can't move in the next few weeks, question whether it should remain P0 — a static speed initiative is a contradiction.

yael-maternity-transition.tldr: A well-structured handover that is now 94 days old — every deadline in it has passed, including Nishant's May 13 India start, so the coverage gap it was created to manage should already be closed. The doc has outlived its snapshot. This P0 likely doesn't need continued tracking; it needs a "did it hold?" close-out and a plan for Yael's return.

recommendation: Run a close-out review, not more tracking: confirm Nishant onboarded & India covered, and that the Australia/India plans landed.
Pin down Yael's return date and plan the reverse handover — the one genuinely forward-looking piece.
Keep the Jul 16 India System Audit alive as a tracked item; migrate it to the relevant licenses initiative.
If coverage held, downgrade from P0 or close — a stale P0 masks whether the transition actually worked.

vendor-optimization.tldr: A genuinely active, well-instrumented Q1 KYC vendor program (Au10tix fraud 70%→98%, authenticity orchestration ~100%, Persona OCR rollout underway) that has gone quiet — the memory doc hasn't been touched since 2026-03-12, ~83 days ago, and the whole framing is still 'Q1' two-plus months into Q2. Two unresolved risks were live at last update and have no recorded resolution: RAI is missing 21% of fraud caught by Persona, and the UiPath sunset (Jul 1 target, now ~4 weeks out) depended on Persona absorbing 90%+ of volume. It does not appear on Yonatan's current_focus, so despite the P1 label it is effectively un-watched at the sponsor level.

recommendation: Get a fresh status from Elad on UiPath cutover — confirm whether Persona has hit 90%+ volume absorption with Jul 1 only ~4 weeks away, or reset the date.
Demand the RAI 21%-missed-fraud remediation plan; this is an unmitigated fraud-coverage gap that has sat open since March.
Close out or kill the overdue AiPrise eKYB validation task (due Mar 21, still todo) and confirm with Vova whether the cross-reference ever happened.
Direct the vendor-optimization-pm agent to refresh the memory doc into a Q2 view and decide if this P1 belongs back on current_focus.

india-license.tldr: P1 license implementation with a hard regulatory deadline of July 16, 2026 (and an internal June 1 SARS submission target). The memory doc was last updated 2026-03-13 — ~82 days stale as of 2026-06-03, meaning the June 1 internal SARS submission deadline has passed with NO recorded outcome and the July 16 hard deadline is ~6 weeks out with no fresh signal. Ownership churned heavily (Yael maternity → Meital → Noga bridge; Nishant due May 13) and the initiative has no assigned_agent, no stakeholders rows, and no tasks — tracking has effectively gone dark on the highest-stakes item in the portfolio (~$25M annual revenue at risk).

Assign a PM agent and confirm the dedicated migration-track CLM PM — the open ownership question from March is still unresolved.
Backfill stakeholder and task records in the DB so this P1 is trackable beyond a single prose memory doc.

ai-academy-product.tldr: The Foundry is a P1 with real momentum through Q1 — dual-track Academy (37 sessions) plus a Community pillar, a set launch path (announce Mar 22-23, lessons post-Passover ~Apr 26). But the canonical memory doc is 82 days stale (last updated Mar 13); only the workspace-memory has moved, last touched May 3 (31 days ago). All launch-gating items — entry/exit criteria, first-cohort scoping with Tal, CISO security framing — were still open in March with no recorded closure, and as of 2026-06-03 every launch date has passed with no confirmation the cohort actually started. Bottom line: either launch happened and the system never captured it, or it slipped silently — both are reasons to check now.

clm-full-rollout.tldr: On-Track to the H1 finish line. The June 3 CLM H1 Strategic Roadmap (CLM Program Office, the live weekly source of truth) shows the program at 54% → 100% by a hard June 15 deadline. China D2P is 100% live (May 10); D2P+Payers has 12 countries live at 50%; HK D2P, Mobile, and ENT Partners (~85%) all converge on June 15. The earlier alarms are resolved or superseded — the real target was always June 15 (not June 1), and the Mar-era UK routing leak is now an actively-tracked 'Redirect 4-step→CLM' workstream. Risk is now concentrated in the June 8–15 window, with India formally pushed to H2.

Close the last hygiene gap: assign a PM agent so this P1 isn't tracked only via the SharePoint roadmap + memory doc.

compliance-data-quality.tldr: The standout of the portfolio — and a rare initiative that's ahead of its record, not behind it. Ido Seter's Q1 announcement (Apr 29) shows Compliance × Product delivered +6.6% CVR, +19% auto-approvals, and 9% fewer manual reviews from six policy/config/process changes with ZERO engineering. Several beat their targets. The Q2 set is already in motion. The only watch-items are tracking hygiene (no assigned agent, PPP oscillated in Q1) and the still-open ORN incident follow-ups — not delivery.

signals.label: Q1 impact
signals.value: +6.6% CVR · +19% auto-approvals · 9% fewer manual reviews — six changes, zero engineering [Ido Seter, Apr 29]

signals.label: Biggest driver
signals.value: CVD/POCA document removal → eVerification ≈ +6% CVR (largest single contributor)

signals.label: Beat targets
signals.value: House-number removal 13–15% vs ~6% target; EIN 3–4% vs ~2%; manual-review −9% and still growing

signals.label: Q2 pipeline (in motion)
signals.value: Weighted Data Points Scoring, Relax DOI matching, Remove EU Bank Verification, Fuzzy CVD matching, POCA automation

signals.label: Open risk
signals.value: ORN production-incident follow-ups still open; Weighted-Scoring iteration depends on Compliance review

signals.label: Hygiene
signals.value: assigned_agent NULL; PPP oscillated 'potential-issues' through Q1 despite the strong outcome

recommendation: Bank the Q1 model as repeatable: Compliance-as-partner-throughout + define-expected-impact-then-measure. Apply it explicitly to the Q2 set.

Make sure each Q2 item has an owner and an upfront expected-impact target (the discipline that made Q1 work) — especially the long-running Weighted Data Points Scoring and Remove EU Bank Verification.
Close the lingering ORN production-incident follow-ups — the one open risk that isn't about delivery.
Assign a PM agent so this P1's wins and Q2 pipeline are tracked beyond a one-time announcement.

partners-rollout.tldr: Live and near the finish line — not the dark, unverified initiative the record implied. An email refresh (Mor Saar, Jun 3) closed a 71-day reporting gap: the CLM Enterprise Partner Rollout went 🔴 RED (May 18) → 🟠 Medium (Jun 3) and is tracking to a June 15 full-completion target. Most of the program landed in late May/early June (Cards live May 31, Mirakl Ulta Jun 1, China/HK ramping). The remaining real risk is narrow: Airbnb 100% and Banking Partnerships ride on the June 8 window, and Cards shipped before its legal question (company card issuance) is resolved. DB tracking hygiene is still weak — the program lives in email, not in the system.

Fix tracking hygiene: set assigned_agent and add Mor Saar / Estella / Ira as stakeholders so the program stops living only in email.

t1-localization.tldr: A second standout — the record has caught up and it's a blowout. Eliya's May 11 announcement shows localized onboarding delivered 4–5× its planned impact: +18pp CVR in the UK and +16pp in Brazil (vs a +4/+2pp plan), with 70–90% of companies onboarding with NO documents, ~2× faster approvals, and reopens cut ~50%. The earlier 'mixed / at-risk' March picture is fully superseded. The standout enabler: platform modernization (AWS + AI) cut new-vendor integration from 12 weeks to 2 — which is what makes the next phase (US → UAE, Singapore, India) credible at scale.

signals.label: Operational gains

signals.label: Scalability enabler
signals.value: Platform modernization — AWS migration + AI cut new-vendor integration from 12 weeks → 2 weeks

signals.label: US validation
signals.value: EIN → eMethods: +50% approval rate, 23.4% faster, +2pp CVR (US) — corroborates the compliance Q1 story

recommendation: Treat the US rollout as the scalability proof point — confirm the UK/Brazil gains replicate before locking UAE/Singapore/India timelines.
Bank the 12→2-week integration capability (AWS + AI) as reusable platform infrastructure, not a one-off — it's what makes multi-market expansion credible.
Feed this into the folded 'What Went Right' retro task (now under T1) — it's the quantified proof; templatize per the retro's four open questions.
Give the SG/UAE/India expansion explicit owners and target dates (the old SG eCollection postponement and the India H2 deferral are the loose threads).

Yael Feldhiem (L&R owner) is on maternity leave; Hila Medina Kraus runs the markets and Ido Seter was asked to cover with scope/cadence still TBD. The Australia EOQ1 deadline has long passed with no recorded outcome, and all three tracked tasks are still 'todo' with due dates 70+ days overdue.

No deadline, no tasks, and no PPP/Q-Plan tracking exist, so this initiative has effectively gone quiet at the systems level despite high strategic stakes.

dlc-ai-era.tldr: v1 of the DLC framework (document + visual) was completed Mar 4, 2026, but the initiative has gone silent since — the memory doc hasn't been touched in ~91 days. The single remaining gate, review by co-owner Mor Lalush-Regev and Tal Arnon, was still pending as of the Apr 2 current_focus and has no evidence of completion. No owner_id, no assigned_agent, no target date, and no tracked tasks mean a finished v1 deliverable is effectively stalled before its Guild rollout.

recommendation: Decide explicitly: is this initiative still live, or has it been absorbed into AIR²/Foundry/AI-Native Team Structure? Update status and memory accordingly to stop it drifting.
Close the long-open tracking gap — request Cursor usage leaderboards now and set 2-3 concrete fluency milestones against the ~Aug 2026 goal.
Action the not-yet-observed PMs (Meital, Yaniv, Noga, Hila, Nishant): pair them with high-usage Elad/Ido or schedule targeted enablement before adoption plateaus.
Backfill the DB hygiene — add a target_date, the named stakeholders, and at least milestone tasks so a P1 has trackable structure.
